questions.question:
Identify the incorrect sentence or sentences.



English Language: CAT 2004 | Question: 84
options: Raj pleaded guilty of stealing money from the shop.
The court found Raj guilty of all crimes he was charged with.

He was sentenced for three years in jail

  1. A and C
  2. B and D
  3. A, C, and D
  4. B, C and D

questions.question:
The question given below consists of a sentence which is divided into four parts, numbered (A) through (D). Only one part in the sentence is not acceptable in standard written English. Identify that part in the sentences which contains the error.



English Language: CAT 2011 | Question: 35

In the following question, four different sentences are given. Choose the option that contains the grammatically incorrect sentence/s.

  1. He is feeling that this relocation is nothing but the kick upstairs.
  2. Consensus is now emerging at our state and national levels.
  3. A more and more rigorous circle is formed from which one is more and more unlikely to escape.
  4. It happened up on a system that worked.


English Language: CAT 2011 | Question: 36

Given below are sentences, each using the question word different ways. Identify the sentence which uses the question word in a grammatically incorrect manner.

Happen



English Language: CAT 2011 | Question: 37
options: It happened on a system that worked
I promise nothing will happen to you

questions.question:
Given below are sentences, each using the question word different ways. Identify the sentence which uses the question word in a grammatically incorrect manner. 

Gut



English Language: CAT 2011 | Question: 38
options: The prisoner’s letters were gutted by heavy cencorship
The famous stars spills his guts in his autobiography
Climbing that cliff takes a lot of guts

questions.question:
Each question gives a sentence with a part of the sentence underlined that may contain an error. Four alternative substitutes are given for the underlined portion. Identify the choice that replaces the underlined part to form a logically and grammatically correct statement and mark its number as your answer.

Feminism is not simply a movement to ensure that women will have equal rights with men but that a commitment for eradicating the ideology of domination that permeates Indian culture at various levels



English Language: CAT 2012 | Question: 56
options: that is a commitment to eradicate the ideology to dominate that
it is a commitment to eradicating the ideology of domination that
but that a commitment for eradicating the ideology of domination that

questions.question:
Each question gives a sentence with a part of the sentence underlined that may contain an error.Four alternative substitutes are given for the underlined portion. Identify the choice that replaces the underlined part to form a logically and grammatically correct statement and mark its number as your answer.

It is unfortunate that the lure of visiting foreign countries still draws a very large number of our people, who do not seem to be realizing what their own country is and how much can be seen and learnt from it?



English Language: CAT 2012 | Question: 57
options: Who do not seem to realize their country and see and learn from it
Who are not realizing what their own country is and how much there is in it to see and learn from
Who do not seem to realize what their own country is and how much there is in it to see and learn from it
Who do not seem to realize what their own country is and how much there is in it to see and learn from

questions.question:
Some of the sentences have errors and some have none. Find out which part of a sentence has an error, and the appropriate letter (A),(B),(C) is your answer. If there is no error, (D) is the answer.

\( \underset{(A)}{\underline{\text{The accelerating pace of life in our metropolitan city /}}} \)

\( \underset{(B)}{\underline{\text{has had the tremendous effect/}}} \)

\( \underset{(C)}{\underline{\text{on the culture and life-style of the people./}}} \)

\( \underset{(D)}{\underline{\text{No error}}} \)



English Language: NIELIT 2019 Feb Scientist C

Some of the sentences have errors and some have none. Find out which part of a sentence has an error, and the appropriate letter (A),(B),(C) is your answer. If there is no error, (D) is the answer.

\( \underset{(A)}{\underline{\text{Frozen foods are so popular today /}}} \)

\( \underset{(B)}{\underline{\text{that many people wonder /}}} \)

\( \underset{(C)}{\underline{\text{how they ever lived without them./}}} \)

\( \underset{(D)}{\underline{\text{No error}}} \)

\( \underset{(B)}{\underline{\text{but understood little of/}}} \)

Choose the most appropriate option.

Each sentence has been divided into three parts, indicated by \( A \), \( B \) and \( C \). Read each sentence to find out, if there is an error. If you find an error in any of the parts \( (A, B \) or \( C) \), indicate your response. If a sentence has no error, mark your answer as \( D \).

\( \underset{(A)}{\underline{\text{I wonder /}}} \)

\( \underset{(B)}{\underline{\text{how am I/}}} \)

\( \underset{(C)}{\underline{\text{to do it/}}} \)

\( \underset{(D)}{\underline{\text{No error/}}} \)
